# ipmi_watchdog (Resource)

Configure the IPMI watchdog timer. The BMC will perform `action` if the timer reaches zero without being reset.

The provider uses the SMS/OS timer-use slot. Set `start_on_apply = true` to issue `Reset Watchdog Timer` after each Create/Update so the new countdown starts immediately.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `timeout_seconds` (Number) Total countdown in seconds (0..6553). 0 disables.

### Optional

- `action` (String) What the BMC does on expiry: `none`, `hard_reset` (default), `power_down`, `power_cycle`.
- `cipher_suite` (Number)
- `host` (String)
- `interface` (String)
- `log_event` (Boolean) If true, log expirations to the SEL. Default true.
- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `port` (Number)
- `start_on_apply` (Boolean) If true, send `Reset Watchdog Timer` after Create/Update so the new countdown starts immediately. Default false (the OS or BIOS is expected to drive resets).
- `stopped` (Boolean) If true, configure the timer but leave it stopped. Default false.
- `username` (String)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_updated` (String)
- `running` (Boolean) True when the BMC reports a non-zero present countdown and the timer isn't stopped.
